15.reason: [Visited array size is too small; should be at least 20000 or MaxV to match graph size]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Visited array size is too small; should be at least 20000 or MaxV to match graph size]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Size is too small: problem states n can be up to 100, so indices 0..99 are used, but if n=100, Visited[99] is last; however, in travelDFS/BFS loops go to i < n, so for n=100, i=99 is okay. But note: in main, N can be up to 100, so Visited[100] would be out of bounds if accessed. Should be at least size 101 or 256 to match MaxV.]

86.reason: [Queue size is too small; should support up to MaxV vertices, but only 100 allocated]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Queue size is too small; should support up to MaxV vertices, but only 100 allocated]

91.reason: [Incorrect queue condition; should use circular queue or proper bounds checking. Also, tail starts at 1 but head increments before use, causing potential out-of-bounds access]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Incorrect queue condition; should use circular queue or proper bounds checking. Also, tail starts at 1 but head increments before use, causing potential out-of-bounds access]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Incorrect loop condition: should be 'head < tail' because tail points to next empty slot. Using 'head <= tail' may access uninitialized elements beyond valid queue entries, leading to undefined behavior. Also, queue size is fixed to 100, but n can be up to 100, so it's borderline safe, but logic error in condition.]

101.reason: [Potential buffer overflow due to fixed-size queue and lack of bounds check]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Potential buffer overflow due to fixed-size queue and lack of bounds check]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Debug printing of adjacency list; not part of required output, causes failure in test cases expecting only traversal sequences.]

14.reason: [Global array size is unnecessarily large and inconsistent with problem constraints (n <= 100), but not a direct cause of compilation error. However, the real issue is missing initialization of G[].next to NULL which leads to undefined behavior during traversal.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Global array size is unnecessarily large and inconsistent with problem constraints (n <= 100), but not a direct cause of compilation error. However, the real issue is missing initialization of G[].next to NULL which leads to undefined behavior during traversal.]

61.reason: [G[v1].next is used uninitialized. Global arrays are zero-initialized in C, so this may actually be safe (since static storage duration => initialized to 0/NULL). So this might not be the issue.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [G[v1].next is used uninitialized. Global arrays are zero-initialized in C, so this may actually be safe (since static storage duration => initialized to 0/NULL). So this might not be the issue.]
